errors: The ELM implementation has several discrepancies with the CPG requirements. 1. The CPG states the minimum age is 18, while the ELM implementation uses 25. 2. The CPG specifies a lookback period of 12 months, but the ELM implementation uses 6 months. 3. The CPG’s “No Screening Needed If” criteria is implemented as “NOT (EXISTS (EncounterLookBack(Retrieve Encounter where type in ValueSet "Encounter to Screen for Blood Pressure VS", 6 months)))” which is equivalent to 6 months, not 12 months as specified in the CPG.
The ELM implementation has several mismatches with the CPG requirements.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG requires Age >= 15 years AND Age <= 65 years, while the ELM implementation uses Age >= 30 years. This is a critical difference in the inclusion criteria and will lead to incorrect screening recommendations.  The time interval is correct (1 year), but the age threshold is not.

The ELM implementation has a mismatch in the age threshold. The CPG states “Age >= 18 years”, while the ELM implementation states “Age >= 25 years”. This violates the inclusion criteria specified in the CPG.  Additionally, the “Has Recent Alcohol Screening” logic is overly restrictive. The CPG states “No Screening Needed If: An alcohol misuse screening (from "Alcohol Use Screening" value set) has been performed within the past 1 year with a result present”. The ELM logic only checks if *any* observation exists in the value set within the past year, regardless of the result. It doesn't account for a "result present" condition.
